channel.title: CyberNetSec - IoT Security
channel.description: Latest iot security threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: MITRE Warns of New Cyber Risks in AI and Cloud-Connected Medical Devices
item.description:
A new discussion paper from MITRE warns that the rapid integration of emerging technologies like AI, cloud computing, and post-quantum cryptography into medical devices is creating new and complex cybersecurity risks that could directly impact patient safety. The report, "Cybersecurity Risk Analysis...

Severity: Informational

Categories: IoT Security, Policy and Compliance, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Mirai Botnet Exploits Critical Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers for DDoS Attacks
item.description:
A new variant of the Mirai botnet is actively exploiting a year-old, high-severity command injection vulnerability (CVE-2025-29635, CVSS 8.8) affecting discontinued D-Link DIR-823X routers. According to Akamai, attackers are using the flaw to achieve remote code execution, download a shell script, a...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Vulnerability, IoT Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'BRIDGE:BREAK' Vulnerabilities Expose Thousands of Serial-to-IP Converters
item.description:
Researchers at Forescout have discovered 22 vulnerabilities, collectively named 'BRIDGE:BREAK,' in popular serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex. These devices, which bridge legacy OT/ICS equipment to modern IP networks, are affected by flaws that could lead to remote code execution, auth...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Industrial Control Systems, IoT Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Smart Buildings, Dumb Security: Claroty Warns New Standard Exposes BMS to Remote Attack
item.description:
Research from Claroty's Team82 has uncovered significant cybersecurity risks stemming from the adoption of the CEA-852 standard, which connects traditionally isolated Building Management Systems (BMS) to IP networks. The standard, which allows legacy protocols like LonTalk to run over IP, introduces...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Vulnerability, Industrial Control Systems, IoT Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Mandates Decommission of Medical IoT Gateways Due to 'Vitals Vapor' Zero-Day
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued Emergency Directive 26-03, ordering the immediate decommissioning of specific legacy embedded IoT gateways used in medical facilities. The urgent action responds to a new zero-day exploit dubbed 'Vitals Vapor,' which poses a...

Severity: Critical

Categories: IoT Security, Vulnerability, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Network Gear Surpasses Endpoints as Top Cyber Risk, Forescout Warns
item.description:
Forescout's 2026 'Riskiest Connected Devices' report reveals a major shift in enterprise risk, with network infrastructure like routers and switches now posing a greater threat than traditional endpoints. These core network devices, which average nearly 32 vulnerabilities each, are heavily targeted ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Vulnerability, IoT Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: "LAR Blinding Attacks" Use Lasers to Deceive Autonomous Car Sensors, Creating Critical Collision Risks
item.description:
A new cyber-physical attack method targeting autonomous vehicles, dubbed "LAR blinding," has been demonstrated. The attack uses coordinated lasers to jam and feed false signals into a self-driving car's sensors, such as LiDAR and cameras. This manipulation effectively blinds the vehicle, preventing ...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, IoT Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: International Law Enforcement Operation Dismantles Major IoT DDoS Botnets
item.description:
A coordinated international law enforcement operation involving the U.S., Canada, and Germany has successfully disrupted the command-and-control (C2) infrastructure of several major IoT botnets. The operation, reported on March 20, 2026, targeted the Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad botnets, wh...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Intelligence, IoT Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Stealthy 'Cuttlefish' Malware Hides on Routers to Steal Credentials from Network Traffic
item.description:
A sophisticated and stealthy malware named 'Cuttlefish' has been found infecting enterprise-grade routers. The malware is designed to remain hidden while it actively monitors network traffic passing through the device. Its primary goal is to exfiltrate sensitive information, such as usernames, passw...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, IoT Security, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Samsung Settles with Texas Over Unauthorized Smart TV Data Collection
item.description:
Samsung Electronics has settled with the State of Texas over allegations that it used Automated Content Recognition (ACR) technology in its Smart TVs to collect detailed user viewing data without obtaining proper, express consent. Announced on March 1, 2026, the settlement resolves claims of decepti...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, IoT Security

Read Full Article →
